files.purpose: Add once_cell dependency required by utils.rs and microsoft.rs for Lazy statics
changes: Add `once_cell = "1"` to [dependencies] section

files.purpose: Shared backend utilities: timestamp, PKCE, state generation, and global HTTP client

exports.name: UnixNow
exports.description: Returns current Unix timestamp as i64. Replaces chrono_now() in oauth.rs, Now() in microsoft.rs, and inline expression in polling.rs

exports.name: GeneratePkce
exports.description: Returns (Verifier, Challenge) for PKCE flow. Uses rand::thread_rng + SHA-256 + base64url encoding. Replaces identical functions in oauth.rs and microsoft.rs

exports.name: GenerateState
exports.description: Returns a base64url-encoded random 16-byte state string for OAuth. Replaces identical functions in oauth.rs and microsoft.rs

exports.name: HTTP
exports.description: Global shared reqwest::Client via once_cell::sync::Lazy. Replaces 7 separate reqwest::Client::new() calls across oauth.rs, microsoft.rs, usage.rs, and proxy.rs

files.purpose: Register the new utils module
changes: Add `mod utils;` to module declarations

files.purpose: Remove MsTokenSet struct (identical to TokenSet), keep single TokenSet

exports.name: AccountMeta
exports.signature: pub struct AccountMeta { pub Id: String, pub Email: String, pub AccountId: String, pub PlanType: String, pub EmailLink: Option<String> }

exports.name: TokenSet
exports.signature: pub struct TokenSet { pub AccessToken: String, pub RefreshToken: String, pub ExpiresAt: i64 }
exports.description: Unified token set for both OpenAI and Microsoft tokens. Previously there was a separate MsTokenSet with identical fields

exports.name: UsageData
exports.signature: pub struct UsageData { pub PlanType: String, pub PrimaryUsedPercent: f64, pub PrimaryResetAt: i64, pub PrimaryWindowSeconds: i64, pub SecondaryUsedPercent: f64, pub SecondaryResetAt: i64, pub SecondaryWindowSeconds: i64, pub HasCredits: bool, pub CreditBalance: f64, pub Unlimited: bool }

exports.name: TokenStatus
exports.signature: pub enum TokenStatus { Active, Expired, Refreshing, Error(String) }

exports.name: AccountDisplay
exports.signature: pub struct AccountDisplay { pub Id: String, pub Email: String, pub PlanType: String, pub HasPassword: bool, pub HasMsLinked: bool, pub IsMsEmail: bool, pub EmailLink: Option<String>, pub Usage: Option<UsageData>, pub TokenStatus: TokenStatus, pub LastRefreshed: Option<i64> }

exports.name: ProxyAccountInfo
exports.signature: pub struct ProxyAccountInfo { pub Email: String, pub PlanType: String, pub PrimaryUsedPercent: f64, pub SecondaryUsedPercent: f64, pub Active: bool }

exports.name: ProxyStatus
exports.signature: pub struct ProxyStatus { pub Running: bool, pub Port: u16, pub AvailableAccounts: u32 }

exports.name: IsMsEmail
exports.description: Check if email is a Microsoft domain - unchanged

changes: DELETE MsTokenSet struct (lines 42-47). All consumers switch to using TokenSet

files.purpose: Add helper methods on AppState and ProxyRunning field

exports.name: AppState
exports.signature: pub struct AppState { pub Accounts: HashMap<String, AccountMeta>, pub Usage: HashMap<String, UsageData>, pub TokenStatus: HashMap<String, TokenStatus>, pub LastRefreshed: HashMap<String, i64>, pub ProxyRunning: bool }
exports.description: Application state struct. NEW field: ProxyRunning (defaults to false)

exports.name: AppState::GetAccountId
exports.signature: pub fn GetAccountId(&self, Id: &str) -> Result<String, String>
exports.description: NEW. Looks up an account by Id, returns its AccountId or error 'Account not found'. Replaces 3 duplicate lock-get-extract blocks in commands.rs (RefreshAccount, LinkHotmail, FetchVerificationCode)

exports.name: AppState::CollectMetas
exports.description: NEW. Returns all account metas as a Vec. One-liner: self.Accounts.values().cloned().collect(). Replaces 3 duplicate collect blocks in commands.rs

exports.name: AppState::BuildDisplay
exports.description: NEW private helper. Constructs an AccountDisplay from an AccountMeta + state lookups. Used by ToDisplayList and ToDisplay to eliminate the duplicated 10-field construction

exports.name: AppState::ToDisplayList
exports.description: Existing method - now uses BuildDisplay internally

exports.name: AppState::ToDisplay
exports.description: Existing method - now uses BuildDisplay internally

exports.name: AppState::SelectBestAccount
exports.signature: pub fn SelectBestAccount(&self) -> Option<(String, String, String)>

changes: Add `pub ProxyRunning: bool` field to AppState
Add `ProxyRunning: false` to Default impl
Add pub fn GetAccountId(&self, Id: &str) -> Result<String, String>
Add pub fn CollectMetas(&self) -> Vec<AccountMeta>
Add private fn BuildDisplay(&self, Meta: &AccountMeta) -> AccountDisplay
Refactor ToDisplayList and ToDisplay to use BuildDisplay

files.purpose: Use new AppState helpers (GetAccountId, CollectMetas), add SaveAllConfig helper, fix GetProxyStatus

exports.name: GetAccounts
exports.signature: #[tauri::command] pub fn GetAccounts(StateHandle: tauri::State<'_, SharedState>) -> Vec<AccountDisplay>

exports.name: StartLogin
exports.signature: #[tauri::command] pub async fn StartLogin(AppHandle: AppHandle) -> Result<AccountDisplay, String>
exports.description: Uses CollectMetas instead of inline collect

exports.name: RemoveAccount
exports.signature: #[tauri::command] pub async fn RemoveAccount(AppHandle: AppHandle, Id: String) -> Result<(), String>
exports.description: Uses CollectMetas. Note: does NOT use GetAccountId - different pattern (mutable lock + remove)

exports.name: SetPassword
exports.signature: #[tauri::command] pub fn SetPassword(StateHandle: tauri::State<'_, SharedState>, Id: String, Password: String) -> Result<(), String>

exports.name: GetPassword
exports.signature: #[tauri::command] pub fn GetPassword(StateHandle: tauri::State<'_, SharedState>, Id: String) -> Result<Option<String>, String>

exports.name: RefreshAccount
exports.signature: #[tauri::command] pub async fn RefreshAccount(AppHandle: AppHandle, Id: String) -> Result<AccountDisplay, String>
exports.description: Uses GetAccountId instead of inline lock-get-extract

exports.name: RefreshAll
exports.signature: #[tauri::command] pub async fn RefreshAll(AppHandle: AppHandle) -> Result<Vec<AccountDisplay>, String>

exports.name: SetEmailLink
exports.signature: #[tauri::command] pub fn SetEmailLink(AppHandle: AppHandle, Id: String, Link: String) -> Result<(), String>

exports.name: LinkHotmail
exports.signature: #[tauri::command] pub async fn LinkHotmail(AppHandle: AppHandle, Id: String) -> Result<AccountDisplay, String>
exports.description: Uses GetAccountId instead of inline lock-get-extract

exports.name: FetchVerificationCode
exports.signature: #[tauri::command] pub async fn FetchVerificationCode(AppHandle: AppHandle, Id: String) -> Result<Option<String>, String>
exports.description: Uses GetAccountId instead of inline lock-get-extract

exports.name: GetProxyStatus
exports.signature: #[tauri::command] pub fn GetProxyStatus(StateHandle: tauri::State<'_, SharedState>) -> ProxyStatus
exports.description: FIX: reads ProxyRunning from state instead of hardcoded `true`

changes: Replace 3 inline 'lock -> get -> AccountId.clone()' blocks with state.GetAccountId() in RefreshAccount, LinkHotmail, FetchVerificationCode
Replace 3 inline 'Accounts.values().cloned().collect()' blocks with state.CollectMetas() in StartLogin (x2), RemoveAccount, RefreshAll
Add SaveAllConfig local helper fn that locks state, collects metas, drops lock, calls config::SaveConfig
Fix GetProxyStatus: read ProxyRunning from state instead of hardcoded true

files.purpose: Unified dialog component replacing EditPasswordDialog and EmailLinkDialog

exports.name: InputDialog
exports.signature: export function InputDialog(Props: { Title: string; Description: React.ReactNode; InputType: string; Placeholder: string; InitialValue?: string; OnSave: (Value: string) => void; OnClose: () => void }): JSX.Element
exports.description: Generic input dialog. Does NOT trim the value - callers handle trimming. Replaces EditPasswordDialog and EmailLinkDialog

files.notes: Critical: OnSave passes the raw untrimmed value. This preserves password behavior (spaces matter). The HandleSaveEmailLink callback in App.tsx must trim the value before calling SetEmailLink.

changes: Fix FormatTime: change `if (!Ts)` to `if (Ts === null)` (line 22) - defensive fix for falsy zero

files.purpose: Fix toast timer leak, switch to InputDialog, trim email link in handler
changes: Remove imports of EditPasswordDialog and EmailLinkDialog
Add import of InputDialog
Fix toast timer leak: store timer in useRef, clearTimeout before setting new one (lines 40-44)
Replace <EditPasswordDialog> usage with <InputDialog Title='Set Password' Description=... InputType='password' Placeholder='Enter password...' OnSave={HandleSavePassword} OnClose=... />

Replace <EmailLinkDialog> usage with <InputDialog Title='Email Provider Link' Description=... InputType='url' Placeholder='https://mail.google.com/...' InitialValue={...EmailLink} OnSave={HandleSaveEmailLink} OnClose=... />
Modify HandleSaveEmailLink to trim the value: `await SetEmailLink(EditingEmailLinkId, Link.trim())` since InputDialog no longer trims

files.purpose: Add error handling for clipboard API failures

exports.name: UseCopy
exports.signature: export function UseCopy(TimeoutMs?: number): [boolean, (Text: string) => void]
exports.description: Unchanged interface. Internal change: clipboard.writeText now has .catch handler

changes: Change `navigator.clipboard.writeText(Text);` to `navigator.clipboard.writeText(Text).catch(() => SetCopied(false));` (line 9)

dependencyGraph.batch1_description: Files with NO internal dependencies on other changed files. Can all be implemented in parallel.
dependencyGraph.batch2_description: Files that depend only on batch 1 outputs (types.rs changes, Cargo.toml for once_cell).
dependencyGraph.batch3_description: Files that depend on batch 2 outputs (utils.rs exports, keychain.rs type changes).
dependencyGraph.batch4_description: Files that depend on batch 3 outputs (state.rs helpers, oauth.rs/microsoft.rs changes).
dependencyGraph.batch5_description: Deletions - safe only after App.tsx no longer imports the old dialogs.

assumptions: utils.rs HTTP static uses `&*HTTP` or `HTTP.clone()` syntax for dereferencing the Lazy<Client> - reqwest::Client is cheap to clone (Arc internally)
The Vault struct field name 'MsTokens' stays the same even though the type changes from MsTokenSet to TokenSet - serde compatibility preserved since field names match
proxy.rs StartProxyServer receives SharedState (Arc<Mutex<AppState>>) and can lock it to set ProxyRunning after bind succeeds
InputDialog uses same CSS classes as the old dialogs (ModalOverlay, Modal, Modal__Title, Modal__Desc, Modal__Input, Modal__Actions, Btn, Btn--Ghost, Btn--Primary)
futures_util stream::iter().map().buffer_unordered() requires the future returned by map to be Send - verified that RefreshSingleAccount meets this requirement
SaveAllConfig helper in commands.rs is a local fn, not a method on AppState, to avoid holding the mutex during file I/O
